placement: cloned from claudefarm1 golden base (Hyper-V checkpoint+export, 2026-07-03); container-portable like the rest of fleet-worker-node

model_provider.notes: Local OMEN Ollama MoE, 140.6B total params, Q2_K quant, see C:\work\tuning\ollama-moe-findings.md on OMEN. Sunk local compute, not metered API spend.

notes: PIVOTED 2026-07-03: originally built to run opencode/DeepSeek via the Zen free tier (see git history for the original opencode-zen model_provider block). Zen's free tier started requiring a card on file, so this node was repointed at OMEN's own local Ollama MoE instead — same VM, same worker-mcp-server.py, only runner.json (base_url + model) changed.

conductor_maf.py (the live conductor) has NO concept of dispatch_pool — it's read only by this repo's local test-fixture scheduler (tools/workflow/reference_runner.py). The REAL enforcement is fleet.json's exclude_from_build_pool: true (same mechanism used for claudefarm1): this node never enters the conductor's default load_nodes() selection, and only gets dispatched to via a per-request CCMETA builders allow-list (see docs/conductor-pour-howto.md).
Very slow (~4.2-4.4 tok/s per ollama-moe-findings.md, 22-40s cold load): overnight/second-pass-critic role only, never the interactive/critical-path pool.
Q2_K quantization is a real quality concern per the findings doc — treat outputs as a skeptical extra pass (objection-finder, alternative perspective opposite AM4), not a final arbiter.
